def: adj. Unaccordant, uncongenial, unsuitable, inappropriate; ill-assorted, ill-joined; inharmonious, discordant, out of tune (syn. an-mel, q.v.).
s.m. A small kind of elephant (written also kumeṅṛhiya, kumeṅṛiyā).
s.m. A small kind of elephant (written also kumeṅṛhiya, kumeṅṛiyā).
s.m. The southern hemisphere; the south pole (the region of demons and Titans).
s.f. 1˚ = kumbhalā, q.v.; — 2˚ = kumhlāʼī, q.v.; — 3˚ = kūmhal, q.v. s.f. A certain plant the flowers of which are compared to those of the Nauclea cadamba.

ety: S. कुम्भ+l = Prk. डी = S. ली; cf. S. कुम्भिलः 'a house-breaker'
def: s.f. A hole made by a thief through the wall of a house (syn. seṅdh; naqb); house-breaking, burglary
subs: (-meṅ), To break into a house by penetrating through the wall, to commit burglary (syn. seṇdh lagānā, q.v.).

def: s.m. House-breaker, burglar.

ety: Prk. कुंभआरो and कुंभारो; S. कुम्भकार
def: s.m. A manufacturer of earthen vessels, a potter
s.f. The wife of a potter.
s.f. An insect like a wasp which builds its house of clay.
s.f. The wife of a potter.
s.f. The wife of a potter.
s.m. The long-nosed alligator (cf. magar).
s.f. Fading, drooping, withering; taking blight, &c.

ety: perhaps fr. S. कश्मल 'weak, faint; dejected,' &c.
def: v.n. To fade, wither, droop; to become blighted; to faint or sink (as the heart); to be or become dejected or dispirited; — v.t. To cause to wither, &c.; to blight, to blast
subs: v.n. (intens.), To fade away, wither away, &c.

def: s.m. Saffron, Crocus sativus.
adj. (f. -ī), Luke-warm, tepid ( = kunkunā or gungunā).
s.m. Saffron, Crocus sativus.

ety: for A. قمقمة qumqumat, v.n. fr. قمقم 'to collect,' &c.
def: s.m. A bowl; jug; pitcher; — a vessel in which the red powder is contained which is used at the holī festival; — a round shade or lantern.
s.f. (dialec.) Luke-warmness.
v.n. = kumhlānā, q.v. v.n. To fade, wither, droop; to become blighted; to faint or sink (as the heart); to be or become dejected or dispirited; — v.t. To cause to wither, &c.; to blight, to blast
subs: v.n. (intens.), To fade away, wither away, &c.

def: s.m. An epithet of Vishṇu or Krishṇa (cf. kaumodakī).

ety: rel. n. fr. qumr, pl. of aqmar, 'of a dully or dusky white colour,' rt. قمر
def: s.f. A turtle-dove; a ring-dove.

s.m. (lit. 'exciting what joy!'), A water-lily, Rottlera tinctoria; the esculent white lotus or water-lily, Nymphæa esculenta; the red lotus, Nymphæa rubra; — name of a Nāga; name of the elephant of the south-west or southern quarter; one of the monkey-heroes of the Rāmāyan; name of a celestial being; name of a mountain
subs: s.m. lit. 'Friend of the lotus,' the moon (the white esculent lotus expanding its petals during the night and closing them in the day-time).

def: s.f. A cluster or an assemblage of lotus-flowers; a place abounding in lotuses.
s.m. (f. -ī), lit. 'Ugly-faced'; a hog.

ety: imperat. and rt. of kardan; fr. Zend kar = S. कर्
def: part. adj. & s.m. Making; doing; occasioning; — maker; doer; &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. kār-kun, q.v.).
s.f. Podex, fundament, anus.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa कवण (kavaṇa), from Sanskrit कः पुनर् (kaḥ punar, “who”). Cognate with Assamese কোন (kün), Gujarati કોણ (koṇ), Marathi कोण (koṇ).

def: (imperat. of کان for کون 'to be'), Be, let it be, let there be
s.m. = kūʼāṅ, q.v. s.m. A well; a pit
subs: s.m. The (Hindū) ceremony of worshipping a well on the birth of a son, &c.
The water of a well to fail, a well to be exhausted
or kūʼāṅ jotnā, To work a well; to irrigate from a well
To stop drawing water from a well
To dig a well; to dig a pit (for another)
'To put a bamboo into wells'; to make a thorough search
(-ko), To make (one) look into (seven) wells (as a cure for hydrophobia); to make (a person) do what one pleases, to lead (one) a pretty dance
'To speak in a well'; to speak in a low tone.

def: s.f. 'A bit off the corner or sideʼ(?); a scrap.
adj. Having ugly or bad nails; — s.m. Disease of the nails (see ku-nakhī).
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Having bad or diseased nails; a person who has diseased nails.
adj. & s.m. Of ill repute, disreputable, disgraceful, infamous; — a person of ill repute, &c.

ety: imperf. part of kardan; see kun
def: part. adj. Doing; making (used as last member of compounds); — (pl. of kun), Doers, actors, &c.
s.m. = kūʼāṅ, q.v. s.m. A well; a pit
subs: s.m. The (Hindū) ceremony of worshipping a well on the birth of a son, &c.
The water of a well to fail, a well to be exhausted
or kūʼāṅ jotnā, To work a well; to irrigate from a well
To stop drawing water from a well
To dig a well; to dig a pit (for another)
'To put a bamboo into wells'; to make a thorough search
(-ko), To make (one) look into (seven) wells (as a cure for hydrophobia); to make (a person) do what one pleases, to lead (one) a pretty dance
'To speak in a well'; to speak in a low tone.

def: s.m. = کوار kuʼār, q.v. s.m. The sixth month of the Hindūs, September-October (syn. Asin).
s.m. = kuṅwār = kumār, q.v. s.f. The aloe-plant, Aloe perfoliata, and its fruit.
s.m. = کوار kuʼār, q.v. s.m. The sixth month of the Hindūs, September-October (syn. Asin).
s.f. = kūmhal, q.v. s.f. 1˚ = kumbhalā, q.v.; — 2˚ = kumhlāʼī, q.v.; — 3˚ = kūmhal, q.v. s.f. A certain plant the flowers of which are compared to those of the Nauclea cadamba.
s.m. A weaver's brush (cf. kūćī).
s.f. The tendon Achilles; the hough (of a quadruped); — kūṅćeṅ kāṭnā or mārnd (-kī), To hough, to hamstring.

ety: prob. S. कुञ्च; or fr. कुञ्चिका
def: s.m. A small red and black seed used by goldsmiths as a weight, Abrus precatorius (syn. ghuṅgćī).
s.m. corr. of kūć, q.v. s.m. A nipple.
s.m. corr. of کوچه kūća, q.v. s.m. A breast; the female breast (esp. that of a young unmarried woman; — cf. kuća).
adj. Of or relating to a march.
s.f. = kūćī, q.v. s.f. A brush (for painting, or plastering, or white-washing); a hair-pencil; a weaver's brush; a pointed or forked beard
subs: (-par), or kūćī karnā (-ko), To pass the brush (over), to brush; to plaster, to whitewash.

def: part. (f. -ā), Crooked, bent, curved; curled; contracted; — perverse, cross-grained (syn. kaj).
s.m. A kind of jasmine, Jasminum multiflorum, or J. hirsutum, or J. pubescens; — a name of Vishṇu; — name of a mountain.
adj. Blunt; dull, slow, obtuse, stupid
subs: or kund-fahm, adj. Of dull intellect, obtuse, &c.
or kund-fahmī, s.f. Dulness of intellect, thickheadedness, obtuseness, stupidity.

def: s.m. A basin, bowl, pitcher, waterpot, water-jar; — a pit for sacrifice; a hole in the ground for receiving and preserving consecrated fire; a brazier; — a hole; an abyss; — a pool, well, reservoir, spring, a basin of water especially consecrated to some holy purpose or person; — a son born in adultery, an adulterine; — an epithet of Śiva; name of a Nāga
subs: s.m. A set of earthen saucers filled with boiled rice, &c. offered to the goddess of small-pox.

def: s.f. A tub, trough, bucket (see next).

s.m. A son born in adultery, an adulterine; — (Dakkhanī) a basin, bowl, pot, dish, shallow pan (cf. kuṇḍ); a rich and savoury dish which the Mohammadans cause to be dressed and served up to their relations and friends annually in the month of rajab (in commemoration of the anniversary of the death of their Imām ʻAlī).

s.m. An earthen vessel (for kneading bread in, or for washing clothes), a tub, a trough, a platter; — a ring of earth round a tree (to hold water, &c.); — an offering made (by a Musalmān) to a saint; — kūṅḍā karnā (-kā), To make an offering (to a saint); to prosper; — dicitur cum plures viri, unus post alterum, mulierem violant.

s.m. A billet, a log (of wood); a block; trunk, or stump (of a tree); a kind of clog or wooden fetter (for prisoners); stock or butt (of a gun); a wooden gun (used by raw recruits); — wing (of a bird); arm (of a paper kite); a piece (in trowsers); — curd
subs: or kunda lagānā (-meṅ), To put a stock (on), to stock (a gun)
To shake its wings, to prepare to fly (a bird)
(or tol-ke) ānā, To come down with a swoop (as a bird).

def: s.f. An epithet of the goddess Durgā.

ety: prob. fr. P. kunda; or corr. of kandhā
def: s.m. The shoulder; — a log, block (of wood), &c. ( = kunda, q.v.).
s.m. A staple; a hasp.
s.m. The finest gold (this word is given by Forbes and Bate; but it is, perhaps, a mistake for S. kandala, or for kundan).
s.m. A ring; a large ear-ring (worn esp. by jogīs); coil (of a rope, or a snake); a circle; a halo (round the sun or moon)
subs: or kuṇḍal karnā, To form a halo round (as the sun or moon)
To form into coils, to coil (itself) up (a snake).

def: s.m. (Dakkhanī), An earthen pan or trough (in which clothes are washed, &c.); a large flat vessel in which bottles of water, &c. are cooled by means of ice or saltpetre.

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Having ear-rings, decorated with ear-rings; — circular, annular; spiral; winding; coiling; — a serpent, snake; the spotted or painted deer; a peacock; an epithet of Varuṇa; and of Śiva.
s.f. A ring, ear-ring; bracelet; a small circle; a hoop; a loop; a coil; a curl; ringlet; a horoscope ( = janam-kuṇḍlī); — a serpent, snake
subs: (-kī), To make a circle or circular figure; to make a horoscope (of); to form into a coil or curl; to coil; to curl
To form (itself) into coils, to coil (itself) up (a snake).

def: s.f. A species of stanza in Hindī poetry (a compound metre of six lines consisting of a dohā followed by two kāvyas, — four ćaraṇs of five feet each = 24 instants, 6+4+3+6; pause at the eleventh instant of each ćaraṇ; the last caraṇ of the dohā must be repeated in the beginning of the kāvya, and the last two syllables of the kāvya must be the same as the first two of the dohā; it is hence compared to a circle, or to the coil of a snake, the opposite ends of which unite).
s.m. Pure gold, the finest gold; — adj. Pure, best, finest, &c. (e.g. yěh to kundan māl hai).
s.m. A kind of wild gourd, Trichosanthes.
s.m. corr. of kuṇḍ, q.v. s.m. A kind of jasmine, Jasminum multiflorum, or J. hirsutum, or J. pubescens; — a name of Vishṇu; — name of a mountain.
s.f. The act of calendering (cloth); — bruising, beating, cudgelling
subs: v.t. To calender (cloth); — to beat, cudgel

ety: kund, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Bluntness; dulness, obtuseness, stupidity.
s.f. A hasp; a staple (by passing a chain or iron catch over which a door is fastened); a chain, or iron catch (for fastening a door)
subs: or kuṇḍī denā (-meṅ), or kuṇḍī lagānā (-kī), or kuṇḍī mārnā (-kī), or kuṇḍī mūṅdnā (-kī), To secure or fasten the chain (of a door to the staple)
or khaṛkhaṛānā (-kī), To rattle the chain or iron catch (of a door); to knock (at a door).

def: s.f. A pitcher, water-pot, &c. ( = kuṇḍ).
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Furnished with a pitcher or water-pot; — one who carries a water-pot; — a horse.
s.f. A small tub, or bucket, or trough (see kūṅḍā); a small stone mortar (in which snuff, bhang, &c. are ground); — a (glass) shade
subs: or kūṅḍī-khutkā, s.m. Pestle and mortar (especially for grinding bhaṅg).

ety: prob. S. कुण्डल+क; cf. P. kundalān
def: s.m. A kind of tent (syn. rāʼoṭī).
s.m. corr. of کندلا kundlā, q.v. s.m. A kind of tent (syn. rāʼoṭī).
s.f. = kundrī, q.v. s.f. (dialec.), A kind of vegetable, Bryonia grandis; — a whirlwind (syn. bagulā; bawanḍar).
v.n. = kūdnā, q.v. v.n. To leap, jump, spring, bound; to hop; to hobble; — (met.) to rejoice; to boast; to presume (upon, ūpar)
subs: v.n. To jump or leap about; to jump or skip for joy; to gambol
To go by jumps or hops; to hop; to hobble.

def: s.m. = kundṛā, q.v.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit कन्दरा (kandarā).

def: s.m. A stack (of corn, &c.), a rick; heap, pile.
s.f. (dialec.), A kind of vegetable, Bryonia grandis; — a whirlwind (syn. bagulā; bawanḍar).
s.m. Gum olibanum, or frankincense
subs: s.m. Name of a medicine, Olibanum.

def: s.m. The resin of the plant Boswellia thurifera; gum olibanum or frankincense ( = kundur, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. Stout, strong, sturdy, able-bodied, robust, athletic; — an able-bodied man, a sturdy fellow.
adj. & s.m. = kuṅgṛā, q.v.; — s.m. = kangura, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Stout, strong, sturdy, able-bodied, robust, athletic; — an able-bodied man, a sturdy fellow.

ety: kuṅgrā+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Stoutness, sturdiness, strength.
s.m. = kangura, q.v.

ety: prob. akin to kaṅkāl, and kaṅgan
def: s.m. An ornamental cordon, band, groove, or serrature, &c.; a balustrade; niched battlement (of a castle, &c.); a parapet wall; spiral line; retiring ledge (as of a pagoda, &c.); salient and re-entrant carving or moulding; — a port-hole, vidette; — a pinnacle, spire; turret, tower; — a crest; plume (upon a helmet, &c.); jewel, or ornament (in a crown)
subs: adj. Having an ornamental cordon, &c.; having a niched battlement; spiral; having a turret, &c.

ety: prob. S. घृणा; or हिंसा; cf. ghūn, and khuns; and also P. kīna
def: s.m. Grudge, spite, malice, rancour
s.f. Substance, essence; precise nature; mystery; nicety; — quantity; species; mode, or reason (of a thing); — time; — face, aspect; — sum, extreme, height (of anything).
adj. Spiteful, vindictive, malicious, rancorous.
s.m. = koṅhṛā, q.v. s.m. A kind of gourd, the sweet pumpkin, Cucurbita pepo (syn. peṭhā).
s.m. A catamite.
s.m. A temple (of idolaters, or fire-worshippers); a Christian church; a Jewish synagogue.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کنشت (kuništ), from Aramaic כְּנִישְׁתָּא (kənīštā).

def: s.f. Bad conduct; misconduct; — bad policy or government; misgovernment.
s.m. An arbour, a bower; a grove; — an alley, a lane; — a cavern, cave; — the lower jaw; an elephant's tusk or jaw; ivory.
s.m. Corner, nook; a confined place; a bower; a grove.
s.f. = kūnj, q.v. s.m. An arbour, a bower; a grove; — an alley, a lane; — a cavern, cave; — the lower jaw; an elephant's tusk or jaw; ivory.

ety: Prk. कोंचो; S. क्रुञ्चः or क्रौञ्चः
def: A species of crane (syn. kulaṅg); — a goose (syn. qāz).
s.m. A dialec. variety of ghuṅgćī, q.v.

ety: Prk. कोंचो; S. क्रुञ्चः or क्रौञ्चः
def: A species of crane (syn. kulaṅg); — a goose (syn. qāz).
s.f. = gunjāʼish, q.v.
s.m. = kunjar, q.v. s.m. An elephant, a large elephant (as distinguished from kumeṅḍhyā, q.v.); — name of a Nāga; — kunjar, or kunjar-giri, Name of a mountain.
s.m. Sour gruel.
s.m. An elephant, a large elephant (as distinguished from kumeṅḍhyā, q.v.); — name of a Nāga; — kunjar, or kunjar-giri, Name of a mountain.
s.m. 1˚ = kunjṛā; — 2˚ = kunjar; — 3˚ = kunjal, qq.v. s.m. Name of a caste (among Hindūs) whose business it is to sell vegetables, fruits, &c.; a man of that caste; a greengrocer, fruiterer
subs: A play in which a kunjṛā and kunjṛī are represented
A greengrocer's till; a mixed account; — a mess, hodge-podge; confusion, disorder.

def: s.f. = کنجي kunjī, q.v. s.f. A key.
s.f. A sparrow.

ety: prob. fr. S. कुञ्ज 'a place overrun with plants,' &c.

s.f. A woman of the kunjṛā caste; — the wife of a kunjṛā; — a woman who sells vegetables, &c.
s.m. Sesamum (syn. til)
s.m. Saffron, Crocus sativus.
s.m. The vessel in which the red powder is contained with which the Hindūs play at the Holī festival (see gulāl).

ety: prob. fr. S. क्कण् by redupl.
def: v.n. To whine, whimper, cry, moan; to be restless, or agitated; to evince regret or unwillingness, to demur.
s.m. = kūṅḍā, q.v.

r: kuṅr-bojī, kuṅʼar-bojī, kuṅr-maṇḍlā, kuṅʼar-maṇḍalā, kuṅʼār-bonī, boyī, kunʼār-maṇḍalā, maṇḍalī, kuṅṛ-bojī, kunar-bojī
def: s.f. = H کنرمنڐلا कुंरमंडला kuṅr-maṇḍlā, कुंअरमण्डला kuṅʼar-maṇḍalā, s.m. contrac. of kuṅʼār-bonī or boyī, and kunʼār-maṇḍalā or maṇḍalī, qq.v), The last day of the sowings (in Benares and the Doʼāb: it is observed as a holiday; and the residue of the seed-corn is made into a cake, which is partaken of in the field, and in part distributed to Brāhmans and beggars. Less correct forms of the words are kuṅṛ-bojī, kunar-bojī, &c.).

r: kuṅr-maṇḍlā, kuṅʼar-maṇḍalā, kuṅr-bojī, kuṅʼar-bojī, kuṅʼār-bonī, boyī, kunʼār-maṇḍalā, maṇḍalī, kuṅṛ-bojī, kunar-bojī
def: s.m. = H کنربوجی कुंरबोजी kuṅr-bojī, कुंअरबोजीkuṅʼar-bojī, s.f. contrac. of kuṅʼār-bonī or boyī, and kunʼār-maṇḍalā or maṇḍalī, qq.v), The last day of the sowings (in Benares and the Doʼāb: it is observed as a holiday; and the residue of the seed-corn is made into a cake, which is partaken of in the field, and in part distributed to Brāhmans and beggars. Less correct forms of the words are kuṅṛ-bojī, kunar-bojī, &c.).
s.m. = kūt, q.v. s.f. Food, aliment, nutriment, victuals, provisions, sustenance, livelihood
subs: Food sufficient to keep body and soul together
To manage to live, to exist.

def: s.m. A spear, lance, barbed dart.
s.m. Hair (of the head); a lock of hair.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Blunted, blunt; dull, stupid; lazy, indolent; abashed, ashamed, modest.

v.n. = kūthnā, q.v. v.n. To groan, moan, grunt; to utter a forced sound (as in straining vehemently at lifting a weight, or at stool, &c.); to strain (at), to make strenuous efforts (syn. karāhnā, and kāṅkhnā).
s.m. Name of a people; name of the king of this people; a proper name
subs: s.m. Name of the king of the Kuntis.

s.f. (dim.) = kunt, q.v. s.f. Food, aliment, nutriment, victuals, provisions, sustenance, livelihood

def: s.f. The aloe-plant, Aloe perfoliata, and its fruit.
s.m. = کوار kuʼār, q.v. s.m. The sixth month of the Hindūs, September-October (syn. Asin).
s.m. A boy, a youth, &c. (see kumār); an unmarried youth, a bachelor

def: s.m. = kuṅwār = kumār, q.v. s.f. The aloe-plant, Aloe perfoliata, and its fruit.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀼𑀫𑀸𑀭 (kumāra), from Sanskrit कुमार (kumārá). Doublet of کمار (kumār).

def: s.m. A boy, a youth, &c. (see kumār); an unmarried youth, a bachelor
subs: s.m. Relationship after betrothal and before marriage
To set apart (something) to be offered to a Brāhman's unmarried son (in order to appease the souls of one's own children who die unmarried).

def: s.f. = kumārī, q.v. s.f. A young girl (one from ten to twelve years old, which is regarded as a marriageable age), maiden, maid, virgin, damsel, lass; a daughter; a princess (see kuṅwārī)
subs: s.f. An unmarried damsel, a maid, virgin, &c. ( = kumārī)
s.f. The murder of a young girl.
wety: From Sanskrit कुमारी (kumārī́).

def: s.f. = kuṅwārī = kumārī, q.v. s.f. = kumārī, q.v. s.f. A young girl (one from ten to twelve years old, which is regarded as a marriageable age), maiden, maid, virgin, damsel, lass; a daughter; a princess (see kuṅwārī)
subs: s.f. An unmarried damsel, a maid, virgin, &c. ( = kumārī)
s.f. The murder of a young girl.

def: s.m. = kumārpan, q.v. s.m. The time or the condition of youth, boyhood, youth, youthfulness; bachelorhood.
s.f. A small well (see kūʼāṅ).

ety: for A. کنية kunyat, inf. n. of کني 'to call or name, to dub'
def: s.f. A name with which one is dubbed (e.g. bū-turāb, 'Father Dusty'); a family name; a patronymic, or filionymic.
s.m. A hole, pit, well; a barn, granary; a heap, pile.
wety: Borrowed from English cup.

def: s.m. A hole, hollow, pit; a well ( = kūʼā).
adj. (f. -ī), Of unlucky footsteps, unlucky, inauspicious.
s.m. = ku-path, q.v. s.m. Bad road, evil way; bad conduct, evil course of life; — deviation, aberration; immorality; heresy; heterodoxy
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Going in a bad or wrong road. following evil ways, immoral; heretical, heterodox; — One who follows an evil course of life, an immoral man, a profligate; a heretic.

def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to a wrong way or a bad course; immoral, profligate; — a wanderer; one who is gone astray; an immoral person, a profligate; a heretic (syn. ku-path-gāmī).
s.m. Evil counsel, bad advice.
adj. Unlettered, untutored, &c. ( = an-paṛh, q.v.). adj. Unlettered, uneducated, uninformed, illiterate.
adj. (f. -inī), Of bad end.
s.m. Bad road, evil way; bad conduct, evil course of life; — deviation, aberration; immorality; heresy; heterodoxy
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Going in a bad or wrong road. following evil ways, immoral; heretical, heterodox; — One who follows an evil course of life, an immoral man, a profligate; a heretic.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Belonging to a bad way (lit. or fig.); unfit, improper; unwholesome (as diet, &c.); — unwholesome diet or regimen.
adj. & s.m. Unworthy, unfit, incapable; — an unworthy person, &c.
adj. Badly turned; deformed, ugly.
s.f. A small well or pit.
part. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Provoked, incensed, offended, angry; — an incensed or angry person.
s.m. A large leathern vessel for holding oil, ghī, &c.
subs: (-kā), To die (said of a king or a great man)

ety: dim. of kuppā, q.v. — ˚ī = S. इका
def: s.f. A leathern bottle for holding oil or ghī, &c.; a skin; a vial.
s.m. A base or vile man, a craven, coward, poltroon.
s.m. An undutiful or a disobedient son, a bad or wicked son; — adj. Unfilial, undutiful, disobedient, degenerate (e.g. kapūt beṭā marā bhalā, 'an undutiful son is better dead').
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit कपोत (kapóta).

def: s.m. An undutiful or unfilial son; a bad, or degenerate, or villainous son.
adj. & s.m. Pitiless, cruel, savage, &c. (see krūr); — a heartless or cruel person; a violator.
wety: From Classical Persian کور (kūr).

def: adj. & s.m. = کوڙهہ kūṛh, q.v. adj. & s.m. Slow, dull, obtuse, thick-headed, stupid, foolish; a dolt, blockhead, simpleton, fool
subs: adj. & s.m. Thick-headed, addle-pated, stupid, &c. ( = kūṛh).

ety: Zend śukuruna, fr. śku = S. स्कु
def: adj. Blind
subs: adj. Inwardly blind or dark, benighted, ignorant, stupid
adj. & s.m. 'Of blind fortune'; unfortunate, wretched; — unfortunate being, wretched creature
adj. & s.m. 'Blind to (one's) salt'; ungrateful; — an ungrateful fellow.
wety: From Classical Persian کور (kūr).

def: s.m. Clippings chips, rubbish, sweepings; filth, dirt, dung (cf. ghūrā); — share, lot.
s.m. A fire-place; a (smith's) forge; a furnace.

ety: for A. قرعة qurʻat, v.n. fr. قرع 'to knock, strike, beat; to beat at drawing lots,' &c.
def: s.m. A lot (esp. one cast in the manner of the Arabs, by shooting arrows); drawing lots; the seeking to learn (one's) fortune by opening a book, &c., bibliomancy; cleromancy; wagering, a wager, bet; — something of wood, or bone, &c. which is caused to roll when an omen is taken; a die; a cast or throw of dice; a raffle; a ballot
subs: s.m. One who casts or draws lots, &c.; one who throws dice (in a raffle, &c.)
s.f. The throwing of dice, &c.; raffling, a raffle
To throw dice; to throw in a raffle
To cast lots; to draw lots.

ety: for A. کرة kurat, fr. کرو
def: s.m. A globe, orb, sphere, ball
subs: s.m. The region of water, the water which surrounds the earth
or kura-ě-ḵẖāk, or kurā-ě-zamīn, s.m. The terrestrial globe
or kura-ě-hawā, s.m. The region of wind or air; the atmosphere
s.m. The ball of the eye
s.m. The region of fire; the empyrean.

def: s.m. corr. of kuravak, q.v.
s.m. See کورده kūra-dih.
s.m. A small, thinly-populated town or village.
s.f. A bad road; an improper course, &c. ( = ku-path).
s.m. A circular enclosure.
s.f. Stocks (for the feet).
adj. Spherical, globular ( = kurawī).
s.f. Name of a noble tribe of Arabs (of which Mohammad's grandfather was chief); — a person of that tribe ( = quraishī).
s.f. Clucking (of a hen); the state of a hen that has left off laying and wants to sit; a clucking hen; a blank, nothing
v.n. To murmur, grumble; to cackle, cluck, &c. ( = kuṛkuṛānā, q.v.); to crack, crackle; clatter; — v.t. To crunch, munch
adj. Generous, kind; noble, great, high in rank or authority, venerable; — s.m. Magnitude; dignity, authority.

ety: by redupl. fr. S. कुर्; or onomat.
def: s.f. The sound produced in biting anything hard or crisp, crunching; munching; — adv. Crunch-crunch; munch-munch.
s.m. Corundum stone, Adamantinus corundum (used for hones, &c.); — a hone, a whetstone.
s.m. (f. -ī), A kind of antelope, a species of deer.
s.m. = kuṛhāpā, q.v. s.m. Vexation, displeasure, anger; repining, fretting, grief ( = kuṛhan).
s.m. (f. -ī), An osprey; — name of a mountain.
s.f. (dialec.), corr. of kulhāṛī, q.v. s.f. (dim.) = H کلهاڙا कुल्हाड़ा kulhāṛā, s.m. [Prk. कुढालओ, and कुढालिआ; S. कुठारकः and कुठारिका],ʻAn axe, a hatchet.
s.m. Bad juice; bad flavour or taste; — a spirituous or vinous liquor; — adj. (f. -ā), Having bad juice; of bad essence; of bad taste or flavour.
s.m. Filth (on the skin, &c.), dung (cf. karsī).
adj. Spherical, globular ( = kuraʼī).

ety: inf. n. of قرب 'to draw near,' &c.
def: s.m. Nearness, approach, propinquity, proximity, adjacency, vicinity, neighbourhood; — relationship, kindred
subs: or qurb-jawār, s.m. Vicinity, near neighbourhood, environs, suburbs.

def: s.m. (dialec.) A kind of sheep or goat having much wool.
s.m. Affinity, relationship, kindred.

r: qurbān, lit., ṣadqa, balihārī, qurbān karnā
def: v.t. To sacrifice; to devote
subs: s.f. A place of sacrifice, an altar
or qurbān jānā (-par), To be sacrificed, or devoted (for); to devote oneself (for another).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قُرْبَان (qurbān), borrowed from Arabic قُرْبَان (qurbān), borrowed from Aramaic קורבנא (qurbānā).

def: adj. Sacrificed, devoted; — s.f. A sacrifice, a victim ( = qurbān)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قربانی (qurbānī), from Arabic قُرْبَان (qurbān).

ety: for A. کربة, v.n. کرب; see karb
def: s.f. Affliction, mental anguish, distress, grief.

ety: for A. قربة, fr. qurb, q.v.
def: s.f. Nearness, &c. ( = qurb, q.v.). s.m. Nearness, approach, propinquity, proximity, adjacency, vicinity, neighbourhood; — relationship, kindred
subs: or qurb-jawār, s.m. Vicinity, near neighbourhood, environs, suburbs.

def: s.f. = qurb, q.v. s.m. Nearness, approach, propinquity, proximity, adjacency, vicinity, neighbourhood; — relationship, kindred
subs: or qurb-jawār, s.m. Vicinity, near neighbourhood, environs, suburbs.

ety: prob. fr. S. क्षुरति, rt. क्षुर्, or खुरति, rt. खुर्
def: v.t. To scratch, scrape, rake, poke, stir (a fire, &c.); to scrape or scratch up (with a rake or other instrument); to grub up or out, to explore; to scoop out (a tube, &c.).
s.f. An instrument for stirring a fire, or for scraping, &c.; a poker; a scraper; a scoop.

v.t. (dialec.) = kurednā, q.v. v.t. To scratch, scrape, rake, poke, stir (a fire, &c.); to scrape or scratch up (with a rake or other instrument); to grub up or out, to explore; to scoop out (a tube, &c.).
s.f. = kurednī, q.v. s.f. An instrument for stirring a fire, or for scraping, &c.; a poker; a scraper; a scoop.
adj. & s.m. Slow, dull, obtuse, thick-headed, stupid, foolish; a dolt, blockhead, simpleton, fool
subs: adj. & s.m. Thick-headed, addle-pated, stupid, &c. ( = kūṛh).

def: s.f. Repining, fretting, grief, sorrow, lamentation.
s.f. Repining, fretting, grief, sorrow, lamentation.
v.t. To vex, irritate, displease, anger; to trouble, grieve, afflict; to tease, worry, molest.
s.f. Vexing, grieving, annoying, molesting, troubling, teasing.
s.m. Vexation, displeasure, anger; repining, fretting, grief ( = kuṛhan).
s.m. Vexation, displeasure, anger; repining, fretting, grief ( = kuṛhan).
s.f. A heap (of corn, &c.); a house; household, family; a house-tax; share, portion
subs: (-kī), To make heaps (of), to pile (corn, &c.) into heaps; to apportion, allot
s.f. A tax on each non-cultivating householder.

def: v.n. To be vexed or annoyed; to be disgusted; to be afflicted; to be grieved or distressed (for); — to grieve, mourn, lament, repine, pine, fret.
s.f. A kind of grass or corn.
wety: Borrowed from the nominative singular of Sanskrit करिन् (karin), from कर (kara) + -इन् (-in). कर (kara) is from the root कृ (kṛ), while the suffix -इन् (-in) is from Proto-Indo-European *-iHnos. Related to کَر (kar, “hand, elephant's trunk”).

def: s.f. (dialec.) A fire-place; a hearth-tax; a dust-hole, dung-hill; — (in Panjābī) a girl.
s.f. An iron helmet.
s.f. corr. of korī, q.v. s.f. A virgin, maid.
wety: Borrowed from the nominative singular of Sanskrit करिन् (karin), from कर (kara) + -इन् (-in). कर (kara) is from the root कृ (kṛ), while the suffix -इन् (-in) is from Proto-Indo-European *-iHnos. Related to کَر (kar, “hand, elephant's trunk”).

def: s.f. Blindness.
s.f. A rubbish-heap; dunghill; dust-heap; heap of refuse for manure (dialec. forms are kuṛṛī, kuraṛ; — cf. kūṛhī).
s.f. (colloq.), corr. of kurīz, q.v. s.f. Moulting (of birds)
s.f. Misconduct, bad manners, improper course.
s.f. A lady; mistress, proprietress
subs: adj. & adv. Lady-like; — in the manner of a lady.
wety: From Sanskrit क्रिया (kriyā).

def: s.f. The state of a bird sitting at ease and in security and preening its wings with its beak; (hence) ease, peace, comfort, quiet, tranquillity, security, confidence of success
subs: (or gulla) lagnā (-ko), To be struck by (the pellet of) misfortune in the moment of security or assurance; to be disappointed, or to have one's pleasure marred, when sure of success.

def: s.f. Moulting (of birds)

ety: prob. fr. A. قرق qaraqu, 'he put, or placed; he laid, or imposed,' &c.
def: s.m. An embargo; the act of seizing, attacking; seizure; confiscation, sequestration, attachment (in this sense qurqī is the more com. form in Urdu); — enclosure; prohibition, hindrance, prevention of access; — adj. Confiscated, sequestered, attached, distrained; forfeited
subs: s.m. An officer (of a law-court) employed to attach property in execution of a decree and to realize the proceeds: a sheriff's officer, a bailiff
v.t. To seize or take in execution, to attach (property), to distrain, to sequester
s.m. A writ of execution ( = qurqī-parwāna)
v.n. To be seized, be attached, &c.

def: adj. Confiscated, attached, &c.; — s.f. Confiscation, sequestration, attachment, distraint ( = qurq)
subs: (-se), To withdraw or remove an attachment or distraint
(with loc.), To set a guard over distrained or attached property; to prevent egress from (a house, &c.)
To send an officer to execute an attachment
or qurqī-parwāna, or parwāna-ě-qurqī, s.m. A warrant issued to sequester a property, a warrant of attachment, writ of execution ( = qurq-nāma).

ety: fr. S. कुर्, by redupl.; cf. kurkurāna
def: v.n. To rumble (as the bowels); to grumble, murmur, growl, speak angrily; to be sullen, to fume; to be grudging or niggardly; to cackle, to cluck as a hen that wants to sit, or as a laying hen).

s.f. A rumbling in the bowels, borborygm; the gripes, a dangerous complaint to which horses are subject: — kurkurī uṭhnā; v.n. To have a rumbling in the bowels; to have the gripes; (met.) evil to come upon (one).
s.f. Rubbish, sweepings.
wety: Borrowed from English cricket.

def: s.m. A tortoise, a turtle; the second avatār or incarnation of Vishṇu (his descent in the form of a tortoise to support the mountain Mandara at the churning of the ocean)
subs: s.m. The fifteenth Purāṇa, which contains the history of the tortoise-incarnation of Vishṇu.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَرَم (karam), from Arabic كَرَم.

def: s.m. A tortoise, a turtle ( = kurm, q.v.). s.m. A tortoise, a turtle; the second avatār or incarnation of Vishṇu (his descent in the form of a tortoise to support the mountain Mandara at the churning of the ocean)
subs: s.m. The fifteenth Purāṇa, which contains the history of the tortoise-incarnation of Vishṇu.

def: s.m. Family, kindred, dependant, follower; race, tribe, caste
subs: s.f. Incest (kuṛum is a contract. of kuṛma).

def: s.m. A caste of agriculturists in eastern and central Hindūstān (being the same essentially as the kumbī of the west and south); a man of that caste.
s.m. The white-crested Kalij pheasant (of the sub-Himālayas), Gallophasis albocristatus.
s.f. Murmuring, grumbling; rumbling (of the bowels)
v.n. (dialec.) = kuṛhnā, q.v. v.n. To be vexed or annoyed; to be disgusted; to be afflicted; to be grieved or distressed (for); — to grieve, mourn, lament, repine, pine, fret.

ety: = kornī, fem. of kornā = koṛnā, q.v.
def: s.f. A scoop (syn. karćhī); a carving or engraving tool, &c.
v.t. (dialec.) To scrape, &c. ( = khuraćnā, q.v.). v.t. To scrape; to scratch; to scratch out, to erase
s.m. A colt, foal (one or two years old, of a horse, or camel, or ass).
adj. Extremely or most generous, very beneficent, most noble.
s.m. contrac. of qurram-sāq, q.v.
s.m. A cuckold, a pimp; a torm of abuse.

ety: for A. قرة, inf. n. of قرّ 'to be cold, or cheerful,' &c.
def: s.f. Cheerfulness, brightness, joy, gladness
subs: s.f. Brightness or cheerfulness of the eye; cheerfulness, tranquillity.

def: s.f. Gristle, cartilage.
wety: Borrowed from the nominative singular of Sanskrit करिन् (karin), from कर (kara) + -इन् (-in). कर (kara) is from the root कृ (kṛ), while the suffix -इन् (-in) is from Proto-Indo-European *-iHnos. Related to کَر (kar, “hand, elephant's trunk”).

ety: v.n. fr. قرص 'to take with the finger and thumb'; &c.
def: s.m. A ball of leaven, or paste,' &c.; a pellet; an orb; disc (of the sun or moon); a round cake (of bread; or of soap, &c.); a biscuit; a wafer; a pastil; a lozenge; any medicine made up as a lozenge or wafer; — a small silver coin (worth about twopence).
s.m. A kind of coarse sugar.

s.f. A chair, seat, stool; chair of state, throne; bench (of a judge); the eighth (or, according to some, the ninth) heaven, the crystalline or the empyrean heaven (as being the supposed throne of God); the base of a pillar; a pedestal; a raised foundation, a plinth; — genealogy; — name of a place within about fourteen miles of Lakhnau (Lucknow), the inhabitants of which are said to be foolish or mad
subs: (-ko), To give a chair (to); to give plinth or elevation (to)
'He is from Kursī,' he is a stupid fellow, or a fool
s.m. A genealogical table or tree

adj. & s.m. Seated in a chair, enthroned; established in office; fixed, settled down; settled in the mind, verified; — one who is entitled to a chair or seat in a Darbār, one who is entitled to the honour of a chair.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کُرْسِی (kursī), borrowed from Arabic كُرْسِيّ (kursiyy), borrowed from Aramaic כּוּרְסְיָא (kursəyā) or Classical Syriac ܟܘܪܣܝܐ (kursəyā).

def: s.m. Cotton, &c. put into an inkstand; a cloth used by menstruous women; a pessary.
s.m. Cotton, &c. put into an inkstand; a cloth used by menstruous women; a pessary.

ety: v.n fr. قرط 'to slice, to cut in pieces'
def: s.m. A slice, a piece; a little; a mouthful; — an ear-ring.
s.m. = کرته kurta, q.v.
s.m. A shirt worn outside the drawers; a frock, a kind of tunic; a waistcoat or jacket.
s.f. = kulthī, q.v. s.f. A kind of pulse or vetch, Madras horse-gram, Dolichos uniflorus, or D. biflorus
s.f. A bodice; a chemisette; — a (soldier's) jacket; a coat.

s.m. Name of an ancient sovereign of Dehli and the surrounding country; name of the territory governed by him; and of the people of that country; name of the most northerly of the four Mahādvīps or principal divisions of the known world (called also uttar kuru; by other systems, it is considered one of the nine varshas or divisions of the known world; in each case, however, it is the country beyond the northernmost range of mountains extending to the frozen ocean)
subs: vulg. kuru-ćhetr, s.m. 'The field of the Kurus,' name of a region or extensive plain near Dehli, the scene of the great battles between the Kauravas and the Pāṇḍavas; — the inhabitants of that country
vulg. kuru-baṅs, s.m. The race of Kuru
vulg. kuru-baṅsī, adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Belonging to the race of Kuru; a man of that race.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit कुरु (kuru).

def: s.m. corr. of kuru-kshetr, q.v.s.v. کرو kuru.
s.f. Depraved appetite, low taste; disinclination, distaste, dislike, aversion, disgust; weariness, languor, lassitude.
s.f. (dialec.) A coil (of string or rope); a thread or wire (of metal).
s.m. A curl, lock (of hair).
adj. (f. -ā), Ill-formed, mis-shapen, deformed; unsightly; ill-favoured, ugly; — s.m. Malformation, deformity, ugliness.
s.f. An unsightly, or an ugly, woman.
s.f. = S کروپتو कुरूपत्व ku-rūpatva, s.m. Ugliness, &c. ( = ku-rūp, q.v.).
s.m. = S کروپتا कुरूपता ku-rūpatā, s.f. Ugliness, &c. ( = ku-rūp, q.v.).
adj. (f. -iṇī) = ku-rūp, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Ill-formed, mis-shapen, deformed; unsightly; ill-favoured, ugly; — s.m. Malformation, deformity, ugliness.
adj. Unseasonable, ill-timed.
s.m. The crimson amaranth; a purple species of barleria; also a yellow kind; the flower of these plants.
s.m. A ruby.
s.m. A ruby ( = kuruvilla).
s.m. The fragrant grass, Cyperus rotundus; name of several plants; a kind of barley; — a ruby (cf. kuruvilva).
s.m. Vulva
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کُس (kus, “pussy, cunt”).

def: s.m. A mattock, a pickaxe (syn. phālī).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کُس (kus, “pussy, cunt”).

def: s.m. = kusa or kuś, q.v.
s.m. A sort of sacrificial grass, Poa cynosuroides; — name of a mythological personage; name of one of the dvīps or great divisions of the universe
subs: (˚śa+ās˚), s.m. A seat or mat made of kuśa grass.

def: s.m. A sort of sacrificial grass, Poa cynosuroides; — name of a mythological personage; name of one of the dvīps or great divisions of the universe
subs: (˚śa+ās˚), s.m. A seat or mat made of kuśa grass.

def: s.f. = کشل kuśal, q.v.

adj. (f. -ā), Right, proper, suitable, good; well, healthy, in good condition; happy, prosperous; auspicious; competent, able, skilful, expert, clever; — s.f. & m. Health, well-being, welfare, safety, prosperity, happiness
subs: s.m. Inquiry after the health or welfare (of a person); friendly salutation, greeting
vulg. kusal-ćhem, s.f. & m. Health and well-being

def: adj. (f. -ī) = kuśal, q.v.
s.f. corr. of kuśalatā, q.v. Well-being, welfare ( = kuśal); skilfulness, expertness, cleverness, ability, conversancy.
= H کسلتا कुसलता kusaltā, s.f. corr. of کشلتا kuśalatā, q.v.
Well-being, welfare ( = kuśal); skilfulness, expertness, cleverness, ability, conversancy.
Well-being, welfare ( = kuśal); skilfulness, expertness, cleverness, ability, conversancy.
= H کسلات कुसलात kusalāt, s.f. corr. of کشلتا kuśalatā, q.v.
Well-being, welfare ( = kuśal); skilfulness, expertness, cleverness, ability, conversancy.
s.m. Unseasonable or inopportune time, wrong moment; irregular or unsettled time, &c.; — adv. Inopportunely.
s.m. = H کسنگت कुसंगत ku-saṅgat, s.f. = S کسنگتي कुसंगति ku-saṅgati, s.f. Bad company, evil intercourse.
s.f. = S کسنگ कुसंग ku-saṅg, s.m. = S کسنگتي कुसंगति ku-saṅgati, s.f. Bad company, evil intercourse.
s.f. = S کسنگ कुसंग ku-saṅg, s.m. = H کسنگت कुसंगत ku-saṅgat, s.f. Bad company, evil intercourse.
s.f. (rustic) kusalāt = kusaltā = kuśalatā, q.v.
s.f. = kuśalāt = kuśal, q.v.

ety: rt. of kushtan; Zend kush = S. कुष्
def: part. & s.m. Killing, destroying; subduing; killer, &c. (used as last member of comp., e.g. dev-kush, 'demon-destroyer, giant-killer').
s.m. An aviary.

ety: rt. of kushādan = gushādan = gushūdan; rt. Zend shu with vi = S. वि+च्यु
def: part. Opening, expanding; displaying; loosening; solving; revealing; — conquering (used as last member of compounds, e.g. dil-kushā, 'heart-expanding'; — mushkil-kushā, 'difficulty-solving').
s.f. Opening, loosening, untying; expansion; cheerfulness (cf. kushādagī); — part. adj. contrac. of kushāda, q.v.

ety: perf. part. of kushādan; see kushā
def: part. adj. Opened, uncovered, disclosed, discovered, detected, revealed, expanded, spread out, displayed, drawn forth; taken, subjected, subdued; open, spacious, wide, ample, capacious, extensive; loose, lax; free, frank, cheerful, glad, happy; serene, clear
subs: adj. Open or wide between the eyebrows; of open countenance
or kushāda-jabīn, adj. Having a broad forehead, of an open countenance; gay, cheerful, blithe
s.f. Openness of heart, cheerfulness, hilarity; liberality, generosity
s.f. Straddling (of a horse, &c.) in gait
adj. Of open, serene countenance; gay, &c. ( = kushāda-peshānī)
s.f. Openness of countenance, cheerfulness, brightness of face
adv. Wide apart, apart, aloof, asunder, separately; clearly, distinctly, plainly.

def: s.f. An opening, an aperture; openness, expansion, spaciousness, extension, extensiveness; looseness; latitude; openness of heart, joy, cheerfulness, exhilaration.

ety: imperf. part. of kushtan; and = kush, q.v.+anda = Zend añt = S. अन्त्
def: part. adj. & s.m. Killing; deadly; — a killer, slayer, murderer.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کُشِنْدَه (kušinda, “killer”).

def: s.m. Horripilation, the hair standing on end from horror.
s.f. Opening, loosening, solving, breaking (a fast); expansion, enlargement; an opening, aperture; clearness, serenity, cheerfulness, hilarity, gaiety.
s.f. Killing, slaying (used as last member of compounds).
s.f. Killing, slaughter; sacrifice.
(prob. = S. कक्षा), s.f.(?) An upper chamber, a gallery, a balcony on the top of a house; a palace, a mansion.
s.m. A palace; a villa; a castle, citadel.

s.m. = S کشمانڐی कुष्माण्डी kushmāṇḍī, s.f. A kind of pumpkin-gourd, Benincasa cerifera; a drug; — (kushmāṇḍ) a kind of imp, one of a class of demi-gods attached to Śiva; a particular religious formula, or sacred text; a certain rite performed as a penance or expiation.
